expectations.text: Correctly identifies that pregen occupies 1 worker thread, leaving 7 for builders
expectations.evidence: Claims '8 workers that can process LOD tasks concurrently' — does not know pregen shares the pool, so misses the -1

expectations.text: Explains that despite concurrency limit of 20, only 7 LODs can build in parallel
expectations.evidence: Says 'effective parallelism is likely capped at 8, not 20' — close but wrong number (should be 7) and frames it as speculation not fact

expectations.text: Mentions the single-threaded DB executor as a bottleneck
expectations.evidence: Never mentions SQLite or database writer. Discusses disk I/O generically but not the single-threaded DB executor

expectations.text: Mentions allowCoreThreadTimeOut or that idle threads die after 60s
expectations.evidence: Not mentioned at all. Explicitly states 'my knowledge of DHS internals is limited'
